# Leadership Report: To-Be-Crosswalked Alignments

## Alignment
- To-Be-Crosswalked: CIOs of CFO Act agencies must report monthly to OMB through October (unless extended), all IT contracts or agreements the CIO personally approves, and any IT contracts approved by CIO delegates that directly support public‑facing digital services. Each agency head (via CIO and CAO) must request utilization and pricing data from current IT vendors (hardware, software, services). Each agency head (via CIO and CAO) must require future IT vendors to disclose utilization and pricing data from current IT vendors (hardware, software, services) in solicitations/contracts. Each agency head (via CIO and CAO) must compile utilization and pricing data from current IT vendors (hardware, software, services) in machine‑readable, open-standard formats in line with M‑25‑05 (open data guidance). Each agency head (via CIO and CAO) must share these data with OMB and GSA when requested. Each agency head (via CIO and CAO) must ensure compliance across all bureau and component levels. GSA will provide best practices, sample contract language and templates.
- Component: OMB M-26-10 - Utilization rates and prices
- Agency: OMB
- Similarity Score: 0.444
**Recommendation:** Leadership should update management of the component 'OMB M-26-10 - Utilization rates and prices' to ensure compliance with the goal: 'CIOs of CFO Act agencies must report monthly to OMB through October (unless extended), all IT contracts or agreements the CIO personally approves, and any IT contracts approved by CIO delegates that directly support public‑facing digital services. Each agency head (via CIO and CAO) must request utilization and pricing data from current IT vendors (hardware, software, services). Each agency head (via CIO and CAO) must require future IT vendors to disclose utilization and pricing data from current IT vendors (hardware, software, services) in solicitations/contracts. Each agency head (via CIO and CAO) must compile utilization and pricing data from current IT vendors (hardware, software, services) in machine‑readable, open-standard formats in line with M‑25‑05 (open data guidance). Each agency head (via CIO and CAO) must share these data with OMB and GSA when requested. Each agency head (via CIO and CAO) must ensure compliance across all bureau and component levels. GSA will provide best practices, sample contract language and templates.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

## Alignment
- To-Be-Crosswalked: In accordance with OMB Circular No. A-137, Strategic Management of Acquisition Data and Information, ( OMB Circular No. A-137, Strategic Management of Acquisition Data and Information, https://www.whitehouse.gov/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/OMB-Circular-A-137-Strategic-Management-of-Acquisition-Data-and-Information.pdf), agencies "should no longer view acquisition data as a singular agency asset, but rather as an asset that is critical to support the mission of the Government." Government-wide sharing of this information will result in more informed procurement decisions, reducing time and cost burdens for both the Federal workforce and industry. Failure to obtain this information for the Government's use in a timely manner inhibits the Government's ability to consolidate acquisitions and make decisions to save taxpayer dollars. As the Administration continues its eff01is to consolidate procurement, it has become apparent that contractors may have information that the Government needs to make data-driven decisions around IT acquisitions. Agencies may be able to request and receive relevant information from contractors for their existing IT contracts, and should, to the extent practicable, obtain such information for Government-wide use. Agencies should actively facilitate the collection and sharing of relevant IT-related acquisition data, in accordance with the measures outlined below.
- Component: 44 USC 3501 nt
- Agency: Congress
- Similarity Score: 0.326
**Recommendation:** Leadership should update management of the component '44 USC 3501 nt' to ensure compliance with the goal: 'In accordance with OMB Circular No. A-137, Strategic Management of Acquisition Data and Information, ( OMB Circular No. A-137, Strategic Management of Acquisition Data and Information, https://www.whitehouse.gov/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/OMB-Circular-A-137-Strategic-Management-of-Acquisition-Data-and-Information.pdf), agencies "should no longer view acquisition data as a singular agency asset, but rather as an asset that is critical to support the mission of the Government." Government-wide sharing of this information will result in more informed procurement decisions, reducing time and cost burdens for both the Federal workforce and industry. Failure to obtain this information for the Government's use in a timely manner inhibits the Government's ability to consolidate acquisitions and make decisions to save taxpayer dollars. As the Administration continues its eff01is to consolidate procurement, it has become apparent that contractors may have information that the Government needs to make data-driven decisions around IT acquisitions. Agencies may be able to request and receive relevant information from contractors for their existing IT contracts, and should, to the extent practicable, obtain such information for Government-wide use. Agencies should actively facilitate the collection and sharing of relevant IT-related acquisition data, in accordance with the measures outlined below.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

Agencies may be able to request and receive relevant information from contractors for their existing IT contracts, and should, to the extent practicable, obtain such information for Government-wide use. Agencies should actively facilitate the collection and sharing of relevant IT-related acquisition data, in accordance with the measures outlined below.
- Component: 44 USC prec. 3501
- Agency: Congress
- Similarity Score: 0.326

- Component: FC 47.200 Seeking Transportation Expertise
- Agency: 3581. Presumption of accessibility for statistical agencies and units (a) Accessibility of Data Assets .-The head of an agency shall, to the extent practicable, make any data asset maintained by the agency available, upon request, to any statistical agency or unit for purposes of developing evidence. (b) Limitations .-Subsection (a) does not apply to any data asset that is subject to a statute that- (1) prohibits the sharing or intended use of such asset in a manner as to leave no discretion on the issue; or (2) if enacted after the date of the enactment of this section, specifically cites to this paragraph. (c) Regulations .-The Director shall prescribe regulations for agencies to carry out this section. Such regulations shall- (1) require the timely provision of data assets under subsection (a); (2) provide a list of statutes that exempt agencies from the requirement under subsection (a) pursuant to subsection (b)(1); (3) establish clear and consistent standards, to the extent possible, for complying with section 552a of title.5 (commonly known as the "Privacy Act of 1974") and any other applicable law requiring the protection and confidentiality of individually identifiable information; and (4) require a transparent process for statistical agencies and units to request data assets from agencies and for agencies to respond to such requests. (d) Rule of Construction .-Nothing in this section may be construed as altering existing intellectual property rights or the terms of any contract or other binding, written agreement. (Added Pub. L. 115-435, title III, 303(a), Jan. 14, 2019, 132 Stat. 5554 .) Editorial Notes The date of the enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (b)(2), is the date of enactment of Pub. L. 115-435, which was approved Jan. 14, 2019. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Section effective 180 days after Jan. 14, 2019, see section 403 of Pub. L. 115-435, set out as an of 2019 Amendment note under section 306 of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.
- Similarity Score: 0.352
**Recommendation:** Leadership should update management of the component 'FC 47.200 Seeking Transportation Expertise' to ensure compliance with the goal: 'In accordance with OMB Circular No. A-137, Strategic Management of Acquisition Data and Information, ( OMB Circular No. A-137, Strategic Management of Acquisition Data and Information, https://www.whitehouse.gov/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/OMB-Circular-A-137-Strategic-Management-of-Acquisition-Data-and-Information.pdf), agencies "should no longer view acquisition data as a singular agency asset, but rather as an asset that is critical to support the mission of the Government." Government-wide sharing of this information will result in more informed procurement decisions, reducing time and cost burdens for both the Federal workforce and industry. Failure to obtain this information for the Government's use in a timely manner inhibits the Government's ability to consolidate acquisitions and make decisions to save taxpayer dollars. As the Administration continues its eff01is to consolidate procurement, it has become apparent that contractors may have information that the Government needs to make data-driven decisions around IT acquisitions. Agencies may be able to request and receive relevant information from contractors for their existing IT contracts, and should, to the extent practicable, obtain such information for Government-wide use. Agencies should actively facilitate the collection and sharing of relevant IT-related acquisition data, in accordance with the measures outlined below.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

Agencies may be able to request and receive relevant information from contractors for their existing IT contracts, and should, to the extent practicable, obtain such information for Government-wide use. Agencies should actively facilitate the collection and sharing of relevant IT-related acquisition data, in accordance with the measures outlined below.
- Component: OMB M 17 12 Preparing for and Responding to a Breach of Personally Identifiable Information
- Agency: OMB
- Similarity Score: 0.322

Government-wide sharing of this information will result in more informed procurement decisions, reducing time and cost burdens for both the Federal workforce and industry. Failure to obtain this information for the Government's use in a timely manner inhibits the Government's ability to consolidate acquisitions and make decisions to save taxpayer dollars. ## Alignment
- To-Be-Crosswalked: Empowering CIOs as Strategic Partners in Agency Decisions The CIOs of CFO Act agencies are required by law to monitor the performance of their agencies' IT programs, evaluate the performance of those programs, and advise the agency head on whether to continue, modify, or terminate a program or project (as per 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)). To effectively fulfill their statutory duties, those agency CIOs must be empowered as strategic partners who are appropriately involved in any agency decision related to IT. The Federal Information Technology Acquisition Reform Act (FIT ARA) recognizes the critical role of these CIOs and forbids covered CFO Act agencies from entering into a contract or other agreement for IT or IT services, unless the agency CIO has reviewed and approved the contract or other agreement. 
- Component: E-Government Act of 2002
- Agency: Congress
- Similarity Score: 0.333
**Recommendation:** Leadership should update management of the component 'E-Government Act of 2002' to ensure compliance with the goal: 'Empowering CIOs as Strategic Partners in Agency Decisions The CIOs of CFO Act agencies are required by law to monitor the performance of their agencies' IT programs, evaluate the performance of those programs, and advise the agency head on whether to continue, modify, or terminate a program or project (as per 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)). To effectively fulfill their statutory duties, those agency CIOs must be empowered as strategic partners who are appropriately involved in any agency decision related to IT. The Federal Information Technology Acquisition Reform Act (FIT ARA) recognizes the critical role of these CIOs and forbids covered CFO Act agencies from entering into a contract or other agreement for IT or IT services, unless the agency CIO has reviewed and approved the contract or other agreement. '. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

To effectively fulfill their statutory duties, those agency CIOs must be empowered as strategic partners who are appropriately involved in any agency decision related to IT. The Federal Information Technology Acquisition Reform Act (FIT ARA) recognizes the critical role of these CIOs and forbids covered CFO Act agencies from entering into a contract or other agreement for IT or IT services, unless the agency CIO has reviewed and approved the contract or other agreement. '. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

## Alignment
- To-Be-Crosswalked: Empowering CIOs as Strategic Partners in Agency Decisions The CIOs of CFO Act agencies are required by law to monitor the performance of their agencies' IT programs, evaluate the performance of those programs, and advise the agency head on whether to continue, modify, or terminate a program or project (as per 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)). To effectively fulfill their statutory duties, those agency CIOs must be empowered as strategic partners who are appropriately involved in any agency decision related to IT. The Federal Information Technology Acquisition Reform Act (FIT ARA) recognizes the critical role of these CIOs and forbids covered CFO Act agencies from entering into a contract or other agreement for IT or IT services, unless the agency CIO has reviewed and approved the contract or other agreement. 
- Component: OMB M-26-10 - Requirements
- Agency: OMB
- Similarity Score: 0.392
**Recommendation:** Leadership should update management of the component 'OMB M-26-10 - Requirements' to ensure compliance with the goal: 'Empowering CIOs as Strategic Partners in Agency Decisions The CIOs of CFO Act agencies are required by law to monitor the performance of their agencies' IT programs, evaluate the performance of those programs, and advise the agency head on whether to continue, modify, or terminate a program or project (as per 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)). To effectively fulfill their statutory duties, those agency CIOs must be empowered as strategic partners who are appropriately involved in any agency decision related to IT. The Federal Information Technology Acquisition Reform Act (FIT ARA) recognizes the critical role of these CIOs and forbids covered CFO Act agencies from entering into a contract or other agreement for IT or IT services, unless the agency CIO has reviewed and approved the contract or other agreement. '. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

## Alignment
- To-Be-Crosswalked: Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.

The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

## Alignment
- To-Be-Crosswalked: Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.

## Alignment
- To-Be-Crosswalked: Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.
- Component: 44 USC 3561
- Agency: Congress
- Similarity Score: 0.321
**Recommendation:** Leadership should update management of the component '44 USC 3561' to ensure compliance with the goal: 'Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

**Recommendation:** Leadership should update management of the component '49 USC 6302' to ensure compliance with the goal: 'Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

## Alignment
- To-Be-Crosswalked: Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.

**Recommendation:** Leadership should update management of the component '5 USC prec. 301' to ensure compliance with the goal: 'Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

## Alignment
- To-Be-Crosswalked: Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.

- Component: 7 USC 8351 (transferred from 7 42 USC 6-426c)
- Agency: Feral Swine Task Force IVN Report 2024
- Similarity Score: 0.34
**Recommendation:** Leadership should update management of the component '7 USC 8351 (transferred from 7 42 USC 6-426c)' to ensure compliance with the goal: 'Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

## Alignment
- To-Be-Crosswalked: Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.

The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.

Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.

- Component: EO 14284 - Strengthening Probationary Periods In The Federal Service
- Agency: nan

**Recommendation:** Leadership should update management of the component 'EO 14284 - Strengthening Probationary Periods In The Federal Service' to ensure compliance with the goal: 'Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). **Recommendation:** Leadership should update management of the component 'FC 47.200 Seeking Transportation Expertise' to ensure compliance with the goal: 'Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

## Alignment
- To-Be-Crosswalked: Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.

- Component: FC 47.200 Seeking Transportation Expertise
- Agency: 3581. Presumption of accessibility for statistical agencies and units (a) Accessibility of Data Assets .-The head of an agency shall, to the extent practicable, make any data asset maintained by the agency available, upon request, to any statistical agency or unit for purposes of developing evidence. (b) Limitations .-Subsection (a) does not apply to any data asset that is subject to a statute that- (1) prohibits the sharing or intended use of such asset in a manner as to leave no discretion on the issue; or (2) if enacted after the date of the enactment of this section, specifically cites to this paragraph. (c) Regulations .-The Director shall prescribe regulations for agencies to carry out this section. Such regulations shall- (1) require the timely provision of data assets under subsection (a); (2) provide a list of statutes that exempt agencies from the requirement under subsection (a) pursuant to subsection (b)(1); (3) establish clear and consistent standards, to the extent possible, for complying with section 552a of title.5 (commonly known as the "Privacy Act of 1974") and any other applicable law requiring the protection and confidentiality of individually identifiable information; and (4) require a transparent process for statistical agencies and units to request data assets from agencies and for agencies to respond to such requests. (d) Rule of Construction .-Nothing in this section may be construed as altering existing intellectual property rights or the terms of any contract or other binding, written agreement. (Added Pub. L. 115-435, title III, 303(a), Jan. 14, 2019, 132 Stat. 5554 .) Editorial Notes The date of the enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (b)(2), is the date of enactment of Pub. L. 115-435, which was approved Jan. 14, 2019. Statutory Notes and Related Subsidiaries Section effective 180 days after Jan. 14, 2019, see section 403 of Pub. L. 115-435, set out as an of 2019 Amendment note under section 306 of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.

Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

- To-Be-Crosswalked: Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) - Component: FC 47.200 Seeking Transportation Expertise
- Agency: USDA STRATUS consists of a set of Basic Ordering Agreements designed to provide an acquisition solution for all cloud solution requirements across USDA. This includes compute and storage services, integration, development, and SaaS software products. USDA Cloud Computing Policy DR3650-001 requires mandatory consideration of USDA's STRATUS cloud acquisition vehicles as part of the acquisition planning process exceptions require CIO approval. STRATUS demonstrates excellent value to the American taxpayer, providing competitive cloud pricing solution to USDA Mission Areas & Federal Government clients. Additionally, STRATUS represents a major step for USDA to mature into a true cloud solutions organization that enables OCIO to provide well curated, managed services. Through the direct relationship brokered with the Hyperscale Cloud Service Providers (CSPs) under STRATUS, USDA has realized a net savings of $7M on cloud infrastructure in the first.6 months of FY 2025 based on the first $50M on Cloud Service Provider Spend. STRATUS is positioned to further save costs and better allocate resources through further consolidation of cloud spending and category management such as Microsoft Unified Support with anticipated savings over $2.5M, evolving USDA's IT enterprise to meet the needs of the Mission Areas, addressing FITARA, Federal CIO Council's Cloud Smart policy, FedRAMP, and other federal guidance. Deferred and optimized costs through STRATUS enable funding to stay with Missions Areas. Keywords: cloud, ocio, infrastructure, FedRAMP, stratus, acquisition, usda, computing, policy, stratus, Basic Ordering Agreements, cloud solution, cloud computing, service provider, Federal CIO Council, usda 3-10-2025 memo, 3-10-2025 memo stratus, cloud computing, usda 3-10-2025, 3-10-2025 memo

Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

- To-Be-Crosswalked: Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) - Component: FSIS Dir 1310.1: ELECTRONIC COMMUNICATIONS OPERATIONS PERSONS (E-COP) PROCEDURES
- Agency: USDA STRATUS consists of a set of Basic Ordering Agreements designed to provide an acquisition solution for all cloud solution requirements across USDA. This includes compute and storage services, integration, development, and SaaS software products. USDA Cloud Computing Policy DR3650-001 requires mandatory consideration of USDA's STRATUS cloud acquisition vehicles as part of the acquisition planning process exceptions require CIO approval. STRATUS demonstrates excellent value to the American taxpayer, providing competitive cloud pricing solution to USDA Mission Areas & Federal Government clients. Additionally, STRATUS represents a major step for USDA to mature into a true cloud solutions organization that enables OCIO to provide well curated, managed services. Through the direct relationship brokered with the Hyperscale Cloud Service Providers (CSPs) under STRATUS, USDA has realized a net savings of $7M on cloud infrastructure in the first.6 months of FY 2025 based on the first $50M on Cloud Service Provider Spend. STRATUS is positioned to further save costs and better allocate resources through further consolidation of cloud spending and category management such as Microsoft Unified Support with anticipated savings over $2.5M, evolving USDA's IT enterprise to meet the needs of the Mission Areas, addressing FITARA, Federal CIO Council's Cloud Smart policy, FedRAMP, and other federal guidance. Deferred and optimized costs through STRATUS enable funding to stay with Missions Areas. Keywords: cloud, ocio, infrastructure, FedRAMP, stratus, acquisition, usda, computing, policy, stratus, Basic Ordering Agreements, cloud solution, cloud computing, service provider, Federal CIO Council, usda 3-10-2025 memo, 3-10-2025 memo stratus, cloud computing, usda 3-10-2025, 3-10-2025 memo

- Agency: USDA STRATUS consists of a set of Basic Ordering Agreements designed to provide an acquisition solution for all cloud solution requirements across USDA. This includes compute and storage services, integration, development, and SaaS software products. USDA Cloud Computing Policy DR3650-001 requires mandatory consideration of USDA's STRATUS cloud acquisition vehicles as part of the acquisition planning process exceptions require CIO approval. STRATUS demonstrates excellent value to the American taxpayer, providing competitive cloud pricing solution to USDA Mission Areas & Federal Government clients. Additionally, STRATUS represents a major step for USDA to mature into a true cloud solutions organization that enables OCIO to provide well curated, managed services. Through the direct relationship brokered with the Hyperscale Cloud Service Providers (CSPs) under STRATUS, USDA has realized a net savings of $7M on cloud infrastructure in the first.6 months of FY 2025 based on the first $50M on Cloud Service Provider Spend. STRATUS is positioned to further save costs and better allocate resources through further consolidation of cloud spending and category management such as Microsoft Unified Support with anticipated savings over $2.5M, evolving USDA's IT enterprise to meet the needs of the Mission Areas, addressing FITARA, Federal CIO Council's Cloud Smart policy, FedRAMP, and other federal guidance. Deferred and optimized costs through STRATUS enable funding to stay with Missions Areas. Keywords: cloud, ocio, infrastructure, FedRAMP, stratus, acquisition, usda, computing, policy, stratus, Basic Ordering Agreements, cloud solution, cloud computing, service provider, Federal CIO Council, usda 3-10-2025 memo, 3-10-2025 memo stratus, cloud computing, usda 3-10-2025, 3-10-2025 memo
- Similarity Score: 0.351
**Recommendation:** Leadership should update management of the component 'FSIS Dir 14950.1: INSPECTION PROGRAM PERSONNEL RESPONSIBILITIES AT OFFICIAL IMPORT INSPECTION ESTABLISHMENTS THAT RECEIVE SHIPMENTS OF SILURIFORMES FISH AND FISH PRODUCTS' to ensure compliance with the goal: 'Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

- Component: FSIS Dir 1510.1: EQUAL OPPORTUNITY NOTIFICATION ON MATERIAL FOR THE PUBLIC
- Agency: USDA STRATUS consists of a set of Basic Ordering Agreements designed to provide an acquisition solution for all cloud solution requirements across USDA. This includes compute and storage services, integration, development, and SaaS software products. USDA Cloud Computing Policy DR3650-001 requires mandatory consideration of USDA's STRATUS cloud acquisition vehicles as part of the acquisition planning process exceptions require CIO approval. STRATUS demonstrates excellent value to the American taxpayer, providing competitive cloud pricing solution to USDA Mission Areas & Federal Government clients. Additionally, STRATUS represents a major step for USDA to mature into a true cloud solutions organization that enables OCIO to provide well curated, managed services. Through the direct relationship brokered with the Hyperscale Cloud Service Providers (CSPs) under STRATUS, USDA has realized a net savings of $7M on cloud infrastructure in the first.6 months of FY 2025 based on the first $50M on Cloud Service Provider Spend. STRATUS is positioned to further save costs and better allocate resources through further consolidation of cloud spending and category management such as Microsoft Unified Support with anticipated savings over $2.5M, evolving USDA's IT enterprise to meet the needs of the Mission Areas, addressing FITARA, Federal CIO Council's Cloud Smart policy, FedRAMP, and other federal guidance. Deferred and optimized costs through STRATUS enable funding to stay with Missions Areas. ## Alignment
- To-Be-Crosswalked: Beginning in May 2026 and on a monthly basis thereafter, the CIOs of covered CFO Act agencies shall notify the Office of Management and Budget ( OMB) of the following: 1) All contracts or other agreements for IT or IT services that he or she personally approves; and 2) Any contracts or other agreements for IT products or services that are approved by a delegee of the CIO, if such contracts or agreements directly enable or facilitate interaction between the public and the Federal Government through digital services (A "digital service," for the purposes of this memorandum, is a transactional service (e.g., on line form, account management tool) or an info1mational service that is delivered over the internet across a variety of platforms, devices, and delivery mechanisms (e.g., websites, mobile applications, text/SMS).) This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.

- Component: FSIS Dir 4271.2: PRE-TAX PARKING BENEFIT PROGRAM FOR NON-BARGAINING UNIT EMPLOYEES - REVISION 1
- Agency: USDA STRATUS consists of a set of Basic Ordering Agreements designed to provide an acquisition solution for all cloud solution requirements across USDA. This includes compute and storage services, integration, development, and SaaS software products. USDA Cloud Computing Policy DR3650-001 requires mandatory consideration of USDA's STRATUS cloud acquisition vehicles as part of the acquisition planning process exceptions require CIO approval. STRATUS demonstrates excellent value to the American taxpayer, providing competitive cloud pricing solution to USDA Mission Areas & Federal Government clients. Additionally, STRATUS represents a major step for USDA to mature into a true cloud solutions organization that enables OCIO to provide well curated, managed services. Through the direct relationship brokered with the Hyperscale Cloud Service Providers (CSPs) under STRATUS, USDA has realized a net savings of $7M on cloud infrastructure in the first.6 months of FY 2025 based on the first $50M on Cloud Service Provider Spend. STRATUS is positioned to further save costs and better allocate resources through further consolidation of cloud spending and category management such as Microsoft Unified Support with anticipated savings over $2.5M, evolving USDA's IT enterprise to meet the needs of the Mission Areas, addressing FITARA, Federal CIO Council's Cloud Smart policy, FedRAMP, and other federal guidance. Deferred and optimized costs through STRATUS enable funding to stay with Missions Areas. Keywords: cloud, ocio, infrastructure, FedRAMP, stratus, acquisition, usda, computing, policy, stratus, Basic Ordering Agreements, cloud solution, cloud computing, service provider, Federal CIO Council, usda 3-10-2025 memo, 3-10-2025 memo stratus, cloud computing, usda 3-10-2025, 3-10-2025 memo

Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.
- Component: FSIS Dir 5710.1: DESIGNATION OF A STATE OR INDIVIDUAL STATE-INSPECTED ESTABLISHMENT FOR FSIS INSPECTION - REVISION 1
- Agency: USDA STRATUS consists of a set of Basic Ordering Agreements designed to provide an acquisition solution for all cloud solution requirements across USDA. This includes compute and storage services, integration, development, and SaaS software products. USDA Cloud Computing Policy DR3650-001 requires mandatory consideration of USDA's STRATUS cloud acquisition vehicles as part of the acquisition planning process exceptions require CIO approval. STRATUS demonstrates excellent value to the American taxpayer, providing competitive cloud pricing solution to USDA Mission Areas & Federal Government clients. Additionally, STRATUS represents a major step for USDA to mature into a true cloud solutions organization that enables OCIO to provide well curated, managed services. Through the direct relationship brokered with the Hyperscale Cloud Service Providers (CSPs) under STRATUS, USDA has realized a net savings of $7M on cloud infrastructure in the first.6 months of FY 2025 based on the first $50M on Cloud Service Provider Spend. STRATUS is positioned to further save costs and better allocate resources through further consolidation of cloud spending and category management such as Microsoft Unified Support with anticipated savings over $2.5M, evolving USDA's IT enterprise to meet the needs of the Mission Areas, addressing FITARA, Federal CIO Council's Cloud Smart policy, FedRAMP, and other federal guidance. Deferred and optimized costs through STRATUS enable funding to stay with Missions Areas. Keywords: cloud, ocio, infrastructure, FedRAMP, stratus, acquisition, usda, computing, policy, stratus, Basic Ordering Agreements, cloud solution, cloud computing, service provider, Federal CIO Council, usda 3-10-2025 memo, 3-10-2025 memo stratus, cloud computing, usda 3-10-2025, 3-10-2025 memo
- Similarity Score: 0.34

This visibility will enable agencies to better identify waste, fraud, and abuse, and ensure that IT investments are strategically aligned across the Executive Branch. The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.

The top-level CIO for each covered agency shall be responsible for reporting on behalf of the entire agency, including all bureaus or components. Reports must be sent through email to ofcio@omb.eop.gov and submitted no later than the 10th of each month. Each report shall cover the period from the first day through the last day of the immediately preceding month. This requirement shall sunset in October 2026, following covered CFO Act agencies' submission of their sixth and final report, unless OMB notifies agencies beforehand that it will continue past that time (OMB may re-evaluate and revise the reporting schedule and scope as appropriate). See the Appendix for a reporting table. See 40 U.S.C. § I 1315(c)(2)..4 40 U.S.C. § 11319(b)(l)(C). Within one week of this memorandum's issuance, OMB will provide a template for CIOs to use to report approved contracts or other agreements to OMB 's Office of the Federal CIO.'. Communicate this compliance clearly to all stakeholders, referencing this alignment.
